stories.title: Wyndham Clark Wins 2026 US Open — Wire-to-Wire at Shinnecock
stories.angle: Wyndham Clark survived a final-round charge and a hostile Shinnecock Hills crowd to win his second US Open title, finishing at 4-under-par and holding off Sam Burns by one shot. Clark shot 73 on Sunday (started the day with a 6-shot lead) — the gallery mocked his tee shots and cheered his misses — but he held firm. He became only the ninth wire-to-wire US Open champion, the first since Martin Kaymer at Pinehurst No. 2 in 2014. His first US Open was in 2023.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: WYNDHAM CLARK WINS THE 2026 US OPEN. 🏆 Final score: -4, one clear of Sam Burns. Wire-to-wire champion — only the 9th ever. The crowd mocked him all day and he still delivered. Back-to-back US Opens (2023 + 2026). #USOpen #PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Clark shot 73 on Sunday and held a 6-shot lead to a 1-shot win at Shinnecock. The gallery cheered his misses. He made them eat it. First wire-to-wire US Open winner since Kaymer at Pinehurst 2014. Gritty. #USOpen

article.headline: Wyndham Clark Wins 2026 US Open — Wire-to-Wire at Shinnecock Hills

research.facts:
Wyndham Clark — US Open Winner
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Clark won 2026 US Open at Shinnecock Hills, Southampton NY — confirmed ESPN/CBS/Golf Channel
- Final score: 4-under par — confirmed multiple sources
- Clark shot 73 (3-over) in final round — confirmed CBS Sports
- Margin of victory: 1 stroke over Sam Burns — confirmed ESPN/Golf Monthly
- 9th wire-to-wire US Open champion — confirmed ESPN (CBS Sports headline)
- First wire-to-wire US Open winner since Martin Kaymer at Pinehurst No. 2 in 2014 — confirmed ESPN
- Gallery mocked Clark's tee shots / cheered misses at Shinnecock — confirmed ESPN/Yahoo Sports
- Clark's 2nd US Open title (also won 2023 US Open) — confirmed multiple sources
- Entered final round with 6-shot lead — confirmed (from prior pipeline)
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- Clark's winning putt "nestled 9 inches left of the hole" on 72nd hole — Golf Monthly

stories.title: Yamashita Wins Meijer LPGA Classic in Playoff Over Woad
stories.angle: Japan's Miyu Yamashita fired a final-round 64 to set the clubhouse target at 17-under, then won on the first hole of a sudden-death playoff when England's Lottie Woad three-putted from 50 feet for a closing bogey to force extra holes. It's Yamashita's first LPGA win in the United States, the first Japanese winner of the Meijer Classic, and the 24-year-old's biggest career moment yet. Woad's heartbreak story is equally compelling — she led for much of the week.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Miyu Yamashita fires a 64, sets the target at -17, and WINS the Meijer LPGA Classic in a playoff. Lottie Woad three-putted from 50 feet on the last hole to let it slip. First Japanese winner of this event. Heartbreak and history on the same day. #LPGA

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Lottie Woad led the Meijer LPGA Classic for most of the week. She made it to a playoff. Then she three-putted from 50 feet on the first playoff hole. Yamashita wins. Woad will learn from this. #LPGA #MeijerLPGA

article.headline: Yamashita Wins Meijer LPGA Classic in Playoff — Woad's Three-Putt Heartbreak

stories.title: KPMG Women's PGA Preview — Nelly Korda Chases Grand Slam, Hall of Fame
stories.angle: Three days until the KPMG Women's PGA Championship (June 25-28, Hazeltine National, Chaska MN). World No. 1 Nelly Korda arrives having won the first two major championships of 2026 (Masters + US Women's Open). A KPMG title would make her only the second player ever to win the calendar Grand Slam on the LPGA Tour. It would also give her the two Hall of Fame points she needs to qualify. No. 2 Atthaya Thitikul (Jeeno) is seeking her first major.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Nelly Korda has won 2 majors in 2026. She needs one more at Hazeltine (June 25-28) to complete the Women's Grand Slam — only the 2nd ever. A win also gets her to the LPGA Hall of Fame. Tee it up. #KPMG #LPGA #NellyKorda

article.headline: KPMG Women's PGA Preview: Nelly Korda Chases Grand Slam and Hall of Fame at Hazeltine

stories.title: LIV Golf Running on Loans — Seeking $350M as Post-PIF Reality Arrives
stories.angle: LIV Golf is operating on a lending facility from Saudi Arabia's PIF to finish the 2026 season, while CEO Scott O'Neil leads a fundraising push targeting $250-350M from outside investors. The league is pitching a "LIV 2.0" model with a reduced 10-event international schedule and prize funds cut from $30M per event to roughly $5-10M. PIF ends support after this season. The future of LIV depends on closing a deal before the 2026 season ends.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: LIV Golf is running on a PIF loan to finish 2026 and hunting for $250-350M in outside investment. New plan: 10 events, prize funds cut from $30M to $5-10M per stop. CEO O'Neil is on the road pitching. The post-Saudi era is here. #LIVGolf

article.headline: LIV Golf Running on Loans: CEO O'Neil Pitches $350M Investor Rescue as PIF Era Ends

research.facts:
LIV Golf Financial Update
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- LIV is using a PIF lending facility to finish 2026 — confirmed Field Level Media / CNBC (May 2026)
- PIF ends support after 2026 — confirmed multiple sources
- Seeking $250-350M from outside investors — confirmed CNBC/Golf Channel
- CEO Scott O'Neil leading investor push — confirmed
- Reduced schedule: ~10 events internationally — Golf Channel / Golf Digest
- Prize funds cut from ~$30M to ~$5-10M per event — Golf Digest
- Leadership team shuffled ahead of investor push — Golfmagic
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- O'Neil has had 5 formal investor meetings with 18+ more planned (from prior pipeline — less recent but relevant)
- Ducera Partners hired as financial advisor (from prior pipeline)

stories.title: Scheffler's Grand Slam Bid Falls Short — Clark Wins, Scottie Finishes Behind Burns
stories.angle: Scottie Scheffler entered Sunday's US Open final round 6 shots behind Clark, needing a historic charge to claim the Grand Slam (he holds the Masters, PGA Championship, and The Open Championship). The charge didn't come. Clark held on and Burns took second at -3. Scheffler's bid for a calendar Grand Slam is now over — unless another opportunity arises. A reflection on what was, and what Scheffler's remarkable run of major success means for his legacy.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Scottie Scheffler's 2026 US Open Grand Slam bid is over. He entered the final round 6 back. Clark held on. Sam Burns took 2nd. Scheffler still has Masters, PGA, and The Open. No. 1 in the world. Just not a Grand Slam winner. Yet. #PGATour #USOpen

article.headline: Scheffler's Grand Slam Bid Falls Short — Clark Wins US Open as History Goes Unclaimed
